mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative quarterly returns in more than 60% of co-expansion periods, or shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects capacity constraints and slowing output, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during late-cycle phases produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal shows no regime-dependent directional shift.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product establishes a baseline earnings trajectory that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week as markets price in corporate revenue expansion.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive GDPC1 revisions are negative in more than 55% of observations, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4 percent forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: High inflation months are followed by positive quarterly returns in more than 50% of cases, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative monthly returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real personal consumption expenditures during a monetary tightening cycle signals consumer deleveraging and reduced corporate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Consumption declines during tightening cycles generate positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the regime condition does not alter the signal directional accuracy.
